title: Idempotency, Inbox Transactions, and Effects · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs idempotency, inbox transactions, and effects from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a duplicate-safe Go effect state machine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Stable Idempotency Key · changed case · Review Stable Idempotency Key
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go messaging team handles case 2142 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Inbox Retention Replay: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go messaging team handles case 8809 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Inbox Retention Replay.
steps.why: Retrieving Inbox Retention Replay through the concrete a duplicate-safe Go effect state machine case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Inbox Retention Replay, keep Inbox Retention Replay connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Inbox Retention Replay, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Inbox Retention Replay precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Inbox Retention Replay, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go messaging team handles case 8809 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Inbox Retention Replay, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Inbox records can be deleted immediately after acknowledgement because RabbitMQ will not redeliver. In Go, a sync.Map of seen IDs supplies durable cross-process idempotency.
